items.title: New move to change SA's abortion laws to go before state parliament

children.content: South Australia's parliament is set to debate restricting abortion access for the third time in less than two years, with One Nation's breakthrough election result meaning the laws could pass the upper house.

items.title: Man charged with rape after alleged Adelaide Hills home invasion

children.content: A man who allegedly broke into a home and sexually assaulted the occupant will face court charged with aggravated serious criminal trespass and rape.

image.altText: A blurry screenshot of a white ute with an open tray driving on a street

items.title: Man killed mother with tool while under 'supernatural' attack, court hears

children.content: An Adelaide Hills man standing trial for the murder of his mother describes to the Supreme Court how he repeatedly struck her while he was panicked and "under attack" by "supernatural beings".
Warning: This story contains images and details that may be distressing to some readers.

image.altText: A woman speaking to a microphone with her hands on her hips between large grey columns

items.title: Paedophile former magistrate to be released on home detention

children.content: Paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy will be released from jail when his sentence expires next month, despite an ongoing application to have him indefinitely detained.

gallery.altText: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy.

thumbnail.altText: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy.

items.title: Health officials warn cases of diphtheria could rise

children.content: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.

image.altText: A nurse injecting a needle into the arm of a patient who is getting vaccinated.

items.title: A mouse plague has broken out. Here's what you need to know

children.content: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.

image.altText: A close up of dead mice laying on the brown dirt

items.title: Landscape in outback SA 'permanently changed' by recent earthquake

children.content: Cracks in the ground in outback Australia caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake could help researchers predict future seismic activity.

image.altText: Dry, grassy ground with a crack going along the ground through the dirt, with a person in the background

items.title: Snowtown killer's suppression order lifts after 25 years

children.content: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image can be revealed after South Australia's highest court lifted a suppression order made 25 years ago.

image.altText: A court sketch of Snowtown killer James Vlassakis.

items.title: Green shoots after rain give SA farmers hope after years of drought

children.content: Farmers in the Riverland and Mallee are cautiously optimistic as new growth emerges from the paddock following "meaningful rain".

image.altText: Green sprouts of barley are shooting up through red soil with farmer's boots in backgrounds.

items.title: Inquest hears elderly man who died was given another person's medication

children.content: An 82-year-old South Australian man died after he ingested prescription medication that was meant for another person and had been incorrectly labelled, a coronial inquest is told.

image.altText: A sign on a window for CHEMIST WAREHOUSE with another saying STOP PAYING TOO MUCH! and a trolley out the front

items.title: Network 10 to go off air in country SA and NSW as WIN Television deal lapses

children.content: The end of a broadcast deal between Network 10 and Win Television is set to leave three regional markets without access to multiple channels.

image.altText: Sign of the number 10 in a blue circle on a brown wall

items.title: Power network's $10 million plan to combat SA's repeated regional blackouts

children.content: SA Power Networks is investing about $10 million to combat insulator pollution, which has been linked to repeated power outages across regional South Australia over summer.

image.altText: Silhouette of transmission lines at dusk with sun flaring through

items.title: Overseas company makes billions in profit but is receiving millions in government bailouts

children.content: China is "playing a hard game" with its stranglehold on the global minerals and metals market and Australian smelters need taxpayer support to "play hard back".

image.altText: Two large white buildings at a metal smelter by a port.

items.title: Death of Peter Hollingworth sparks difficult feelings for abuse victim

children.content: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth, who resigned over his handling of child abuse in the church, died in Melbourne on Tuesday at the age of 91.

children.content: The two leaders are expected to discuss bilateral economic relations and "key international and regional issues", according to the Kremlin. Follow live.

items.title: Doctor fined for book of 'WTF' surgery pictures taken without consent

children.content: An anaesthetist who worked at Toowoomba Hospital has been fined $30,000 for professional misconduct after he included confidential patient information in a self-published book.

image.altText: A doctor in scrubs and with a headband is standing in front of a piece of medical equipment.

items.title: Greenland tells US again it is 'not for sale' after Trump envoy talks

children.content: Jeff Landry, appointed by Donald Trump in December as the US special envoy to Greenland, has met the country's prime minister in the capital Nuuk.

items.title: Maldives investigates if Italian divers went too deep in fatal cave dive

children.content: A Maldives government official says a permit was given to study soft corals, but authorities were unaware the group planned to cave dive.

image.altText: divers

items.title: Australian government calls for detained flotilla members to be released

children.content: The Australian government says it is engaging with Israel over the welfare of 11 Australians detained, as Israeli forces fire "non-lethal" shots at two boats in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla.

image.altText: a war boat driving towards photographer

items.title: Married at First Sight UK allegations

children.content: A British network has entirely removed Married at First Sight UK from air and its streaming channel after allegations of rape and sexual assault.
The show is widely seen as controversial with accusations it normalises or even platforms toxic relationships.
Here's what we know about the allegations so far.

items.title: Teenager Bianca Adler becomes youngest Australian to summit Everest

image.altText: A girl wearing a helmet with stickets in a purple jacket holds a pick ice pick close to the camera, smiling widely.

items.title: ASX falls below 8,500 level as rate rise fears surface — as it happened

children.content: The Australian share market has fallen below 8,500 points after a US bond sell-off triggered further losses on Wall Street. Oil prices slipped after Donald Trump said he had been an hour away from ordering an attack on Iran before postponing it.

image.altText: Shares on a screen at the ASX

children.content: Before rising conflict pushed the Strait of Hormuz into the spotlight as a choke point vital to economies globally, tourism transformed women's lives.

items.title: Former NT child protection staff speak out after Kumanjayi Little Baby case

children.content: In the three weeks since the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs, the NT's child protection system has come under intense scrutiny. Now, former child protection workers are speaking out about why they believe the system is broken.

items.title: Alcaraz injury woes deepen, former champion out of Wimbledon

children.content: Carlos Alcaraz will miss Wimbledon as he continues his rehab from a wrist injury that has already ruled him out of the French Open.

items.title: Mosque shooting victims hailed as heroes and mourned by community

children.content: Three people killed by two shooters at a San Diego mosque were beloved pillars of the community and died while saving 140 children in the building at the time of the attack, authorities say.

image.altText: Images of three smiling men in separate black digital TV frames, alongside US flags.

items.title: More ISIS-linked women and children set to return to Australia

children.content: Six Australian women, along with their children and grandchildren, look likely to leave a camp in Syria within days.

image.altText: A woman in a black niqab walks along a row of tents, with children seen in the distance.

items.title: Miners plan to retrofit WA nickel plants to cash in on high gold prices

children.content: Idle infrastructure from WA's once-booming nickel industry is set to be retrofitted to produce gold bars amid historic high prices for the precious metal.

items.title: Why is there no vaccine for this Ebola outbreak?

children.content: The latest Ebola outbreak is caused by a strain of virus discovered nearly two decades ago, yet there is no vaccine. So what does this mean for people affected? And can vaccines be fast-tracked?

items.title: Apprehension as UK mining company enters Australian coal

children.content: Anglo American has operated metallurgical coal mines in Queensland since the turn of the century. Now, a UK-based company has bought them all.

items.title: Worm living in a 'glass castle' among thousands of new marine species

children.content: Thousands of species have been discovered lurking and thriving in our oceans, as part of an effort to catalogue 100,000 new species.
